failures.explanation: Direction mismatch in a standard single-gene perturbation. Likely wrong-sign or missing intermediate edge in the PHYB/PRR37/Ghd7 → Ehd1 → SbFT → Flowering_Time cascade. [algebraic=fail, ode=correct, rwr=fail]
failures.fix_strategy: Add missing direct edge or correct sign in PHYB/PRR37/Ghd7 → Ehd1 → SbFT → Flowering_Time cascade based on literature.

failures.explanation: Cascade amplification / collapse (ratio=2.14e+03). Bounded-inverse inhibition on Flowering_Time plus long FT-arm cascade pushes the ratio to the K=10 ceiling or to the epsilon floor — predicted direction is correct in sign but wildly over-magnified, and small sign errors upstream flip the final direction. [algebraic=fail, ode=fail, rwr=fail]

failures.explanation: Single SbFT paralog perturbed while other paralogs remain at WT. In the model FT1/FT8/FT10 all feed the same downstream integrator, so single-paralog perturbation is diluted by the geometric mean / sum across paralogs, producing small effects that fall below the direction threshold. [algebraic=fail, ode=correct, rwr=correct]
failures.fix_strategy: Collapse SbFT paralogs into a single FT_paralog_family node, or encode per-paralog contribution weights on the FT→Flowering_Time edges so single-paralog perturbations register.

failures.explanation: Multi-gene interaction with compounding / opposing effects. Coherent feed-forward arms (PRR37 vs Ghd7 vs PhyB) combine in non-additive ways; geometric-mean activation under-weights the summed repression release. [algebraic=fail, ode=fail, rwr=correct]
failures.fix_strategy: Wire PRR37 and Ghd7 arms as independent coherent feed-forwards on Ehd1/SbFT so that double mutants additively stack releases.

summary.note: Failures are the UNION across all three methods (Algebraic, ODE, RWR). Per-method pass/fail is shown in the explanation field. Fixable=false for framework_limitation; REFINEMENT should focus on composite_collapse and edge_case items first.
